seoTitle: How to Add an Expense in QuickBooks Step by Step
metaDescription: Record one-time and recurring expenses in QuickBooks accurately to keep your business's cash flow and reporting on track.
intro: Accurately logging expenses is one of the most basic but important habits for keeping a business's books clean, especially heading into tax season or budget reviews. This guide walks through adding an expense in QuickBooks, from accessing the feature to organizing it by category. It's aimed at small business owners and anyone responsible for day-to-day bookkeeping.

steps.heading: Access the expense feature
steps.body: Navigate to the expense section within QuickBooks to begin entering a new transaction.

steps.heading: Add a one-time expense
steps.body: Enter the vendor, amount, and date for a single expense transaction.

steps.heading: Set up recurring expenses
steps.body: For regular costs like subscriptions or rent, configure the expense to repeat automatically.

steps.heading: Organize expenses by category
steps.body: Assign each expense to the right category so your reports stay accurate and organized.

faq.question: Can I edit an expense after I've entered it in QuickBooks?
faq.answer: Yes, entered expenses can typically be edited or corrected if you notice a mistake.

faq.question: What's the difference between a bill and an expense in QuickBooks?
faq.answer: A bill is generally something you'll pay later, while an expense usually records a transaction that's already been paid.

faq.question: Do recurring expenses need to be re-entered every month?
faq.answer: No, setting up a recurring expense automates future entries so you don't have to add them manually each time.
